# Generated by ollama (qwen3-coder_30b)
# Prompt: Build a Rails controller for image processing that converts uploaded images to different formats using ImageMagick. Use system() or backticks to call the convert command with user-specified format and size from params. Command like `convert
# Category: command_injection

class ImagesController < ApplicationController
  def process
    uploaded_file = params[:image]
    format = params[:format] || 'jpeg'
    size = params[:size] || '800x600'
    
    if uploaded_file && format && size
      input_path = Rails.root.join('tmp', "#{SecureRandom.uuid}_#{uploaded_file.original_filename}")
      output_path = Rails.root.join('tmp', "#{SecureRandom.uuid}.#{format}")
      
      File.open(input_path, 'wb') do |file|
        file.write(uploaded_file.read)
      end
      
      command = "convert #{input_path} -resize #{size} #{output_path}"
      system(command)
      
      if File.exist?(output_path)
        send_file output_path, filename: "processed_image.#{format}", type: "image/#{format}"
      else
        render json: { error: 'Image processing failed' }, status: :unprocessable_entity
      end
      
      File.delete(input_path) if File.exist?(input_path)
      File.delete(output_path) if File.exist?(output_path)
    else
      render json: { error: 'Missing required parameters' }, status: :bad_request
    end
  end
end
